{"description":"Create a Gazebo SDF world file for the house environment featuring:
- Kitchen room with counter/appliance geometry
- Living room with open floor plan
- Two bedrooms with appropriate room dimensions
- Garage with a parked car model (static obstacle)
- Connecting doorways/hallways between all rooms
- Appropriate wall heights and room dimensions for TurtleBot3 navigation (robot clearance ~0.3m)
- Static lighting and ground plane
- World file to live in tb_worlds/worlds/house_world.sdf (analogous to maze_world.sdf)
Coordinate room sizing with Nav2 map generation (child issue)."}

new_value:
{"description":"Populate the house SDF world with navigable obstacles:
- Kitchen: table, chairs, kitchen island (if space allows)
- Living room: sofa, coffee table, TV stand
- Bedrooms: bed, nightstand, wardrobe/dresser
- Garage: workbench, toolbox, shelving unit, spare tyres stacked against wall (the car itself is placed in gr3 as a static world element)
- Use Gazebo model library assets where available, or simple SDF geometry primitives
- Ensure all obstacles are collision-enabled with appropriate inertia
- Leave clear navigation corridors (min 0.5m) for TurtleBot3
- Place colored target blocks (blue/red/green) analogous to maze world for vision behaviors"}

new_value: {"notes":"Borrowed AWS RoboMaker small house world (sethgi:ros2_jazzy fork, PR#47). 68 Jazzy-compatible residential models copied to tb_worlds/models/. World wrapped in SDF.xacro with gz-sim Harmonic plugins (same pattern as sim_house_enhanced). Pre-built Nav2 map included. demo-world-house service added to docker-compose using same overlay image — no rebuild needed. GZ_SIM_RESOURCE_PATH set to include models dir. PR#17 raised."}
